armed-conflicts.headline: US-Iran war continues with stalled peace talks and Strait of Hormuz disruption.
armed-conflicts.summary: On May 17, 2026, the ongoing US-Iran conflict remains a significant concern, with peace talks mediated by Pakistan reportedly stalled despite a fragile ceasefire. The continued disruption of the Strait of Hormuz is severely impacting global oil supplies and international traffic. Israel has also voiced its stance on the negotiations, emphasizing the need for direct involvement and a tougher approach.

regions.name: Tehran, Iran
regions.detail: Iran has stated that transit through the Strait of Hormuz will flow once the conflict with the US and Israel is over.

regions.name: Washington D.C., United States
regions.detail: The US is weighing fresh strikes on Iran under 'Operation Sledgehammer' while participating in stalled peace talks.

regions.name: Islamabad, Pakistan
regions.detail: Pakistan is actively mediating peace talks between Iran and the United States, expressing optimism for a sustainable agreement.

regions.name: Jerusalem, Israel
regions.detail: Israel believes that present negotiations between the US and Iran are not progressing and advocates for a stronger stance.

regions.name: Strait of Hormuz
regions.detail: The de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz by Iran is characterized as the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.

human-rights.headline: Global concerns over digital surveillance and LGBTIQ+ rights persist.
human-rights.summary: May 17, 2026, marks the International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia, and Biphobia (IDAHOBIT), with organizations worldwide highlighting rising discrimination, violence, and criminalization against LGBTIQ+ people. Concurrently, UN experts have raised concerns about the growing global spread of intrusive digital surveillance technologies and the misuse of AI, which undermine fundamental freedoms and civic space.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland
regions.detail: UN experts in Geneva have issued a joint statement raising concerns over the global spread of intrusive digital surveillance technologies and AI misuse.

regions.name: Brussels, Belgium
regions.detail: EU proposals to 'simplify' tech laws are being warned by civil society as potentially rolling back rights and weakening data protection for AI systems.

regions.name: Budapest, Hungary
regions.detail: Legislative changes in Hungary have enabled the use of facial recognition technology targeting peaceful assemblies like Pride Marches.

regions.name: Hartford, Connecticut, USA
regions.detail: Connecticut lawmakers approved legislation targeting youth social media addiction and introducing guardrails for AI tools.

climate-events.headline: Rivers losing oxygen globally; Pan-European region heats fastest.
climate-events.summary: A new study reveals that global warming is causing rivers worldwide to slowly lose oxygen, threatening aquatic life, with eastern US, India, and tropical rivers particularly vulnerable. Additionally, the Pan-European region is experiencing temperatures rising at twice the global average, prompting a 'Call to Action' from the Pan-European Commission on Climate and Health highlighting the immediate crisis.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland
regions.detail: The Pan-European Commission on Climate and Health, convened by WHO Regional Director for Europe, published a Call to Action on May 17, 2026, regarding the accelerating climate crisis.

regions.name: Washington D.C., United States
regions.detail: Rivers in the eastern US are projected to lose about 10% of their oxygen by the end of the century due to global warming.

regions.name: New Delhi, India
regions.detail: India's heavily polluted Ganges River was previously losing oxygen more than 20 times faster than the global average, and other rivers face significant oxygen loss.

regions.name: Kimitsu, Chiba Prefecture, Japan
regions.detail: Japan's new mandatory emissions trading program, GX-ETS, aims to accelerate efforts to reach net zero by 2050, building on a positive trend of emissions reduction.

regions.name: Manaus, Brazil
regions.detail: Scientists express worry about tropical rivers like the Amazon, where the number of days with dead zone spots has risen.

public-health.headline: Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda declared public health emergency.
public-health.summary: On May 17, 2026, the World Health Organization (WHO) declared an Ebola outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo virus, in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a 'public health emergency of international concern'. The outbreak has led to at least 80 suspected deaths in DRC's Ituri province, with confirmed cases also reported in Kampala, Uganda, and Kinshasa, DRC.

regions.name: Bunia, Democratic Republic of the Congo
regions.detail: The Ituri province, including Bunia, is a major area affected by the Ebola outbreak, with 80 suspected deaths and 246 suspected cases reported.

regions.name: Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo
regions.detail: The first cases of the Ebola outbreak were reported in the Mongwalu health zone, a high-traffic mining area in Ituri province.

regions.name: Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of the Congo
regions.detail: A laboratory-confirmed Ebola case was reported in Kinshasa from an individual returning from Ituri.

regions.name: Kampala, Uganda
regions.detail: Two laboratory-confirmed Ebola cases, including one death, were reported in Kampala, linked to individuals traveling from the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland
regions.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) Director-General, based in Geneva, declared the Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern.

economic-crises.headline: Global economy hit by Iran war, inflation, and Strait of Hormuz disruption.
economic-crises.summary: The ongoing war in Iran is causing significant global economic disruption, fueling inflation and increasing oil prices by approximately 50% compared to pre-war levels. The de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz has been a major supply disruption, pushing millions towards poverty due to rising fuel and food costs. Meanwhile, the US and China have achieved positive outcomes in economic and trade consultations, including agreements on tariff arrangements and trade councils.

regions.name: Global
regions.detail: The global economy is experiencing disruption and rising inflation due to the war in Iran and increased oil prices.

regions.name: Strait of Hormuz
regions.detail: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has caused the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.

regions.name: Beijing, China
regions.detail: China and the United States have achieved positive outcomes in economic and trade consultations, agreeing on tariff arrangements and trade councils.

regions.name: Washington D.C., United States
regions.detail: The US has engaged in economic and trade consultations with China, reaching positive consensus on various issues.

regions.name: Karachi, Pakistan
regions.detail: The Pakistan Stock Exchange experienced a 3.2% drop as uncertainty over US-Iran peace negotiations weighed on investor sentiment.

regions.name: Dhaka, Bangladesh
regions.detail: Bangladesh faces recession-like conditions, impacted by rising fuel and transport costs.

democratic-erosion.headline: IDAHOBIT highlights LGBTIQ+ rights at the heart of democracy; Taiwan's status debated.
democratic-erosion.summary: On May 17, 2026, the International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia, and Biphobia (IDAHOBIT) is celebrated under the theme 'At the heart of democracy,' drawing attention to rising discrimination and criminalization against LGBTIQ+ people and their impact on democratic systems. Concurrently, Taiwan reaffirms its commitment to maintaining the cross-strait 'status quo' as a sovereign democratic nation, amid discussions and comments from US President Donald Trump regarding arms supplies and Taiwan's independence.

regions.name: Global
regions.detail: The celebration of IDAHOBIT on May 17 highlights the link between LGBTIQ+ rights and the health of democratic processes worldwide.

regions.name: Taipei, Taiwan
regions.detail: Taiwan's Presidential Office reiterated its commitment to preserving the cross-strait 'status quo' as a sovereign and independent democratic nation.

regions.name: Beijing, China
regions.detail: Chinese President Xi Jinping pressed US President Trump not to support Taiwan's independence during their summit.

regions.name: Washington D.C., United States
regions.detail: US President Donald Trump made comments regarding Taiwan's independence and arms supplies, maintaining a policy of strategic ambiguity.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland
regions.detail: UN bodies emphasize that democracies are strongest when inclusive, with concerns raised over rising intolerance against LGBTIQ+ people.

humanitarian.headline: US pledges $1.8B in aid, but UN agencies remain overstretched amidst rising needs.
humanitarian.summary: On May 17, 2026, the United States announced an additional $1.8 billion in funding for UN humanitarian aid efforts, bringing the total recent US support to $3.8 billion. Despite this, UN humanitarian agencies remain 'overstretched, underresourced and literally under attack' as global needs continue to outpace available resources. The funding aims to support 21 countries, including Ukraine, Lebanon, Venezuela, and the Central African Republic.

regions.name: New York, United States
regions.detail: The US pledged an additional $1.8 billion in humanitarian funding to the UN, announced by the Trump administration.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland
regions.detail: The UN Secretary-General welcomed the US contribution, as humanitarian agencies face significant funding shortfalls.

regions.name: Kyiv, Ukraine
regions.detail: Ukraine is among the 21 countries targeted to receive humanitarian aid from the new US funding.

regions.name: Beirut, Lebanon
regions.detail: Lebanon is one of the countries that will receive support from the US humanitarian aid package.

regions.name: Caracas, Venezuela
regions.detail: Venezuela is included in the list of countries benefiting from the additional US humanitarian funding.

regions.name: Bangui, Central African Republic
regions.detail: The Central African Republic is a recipient of the US humanitarian aid, aimed at addressing urgent needs.
